operating_context.status: Northstar partner meeting is tomorrow; missing materials are the top founder-owned blocker.
Atlas needs a mitigation plan this week or the pilot is at risk of losing momentum.
Calendar permission scope is blocking the onboarding checklist automation path.
All demos should remain synthetic, local-only, and approval-gated until explicit source approval exists.

meeting_preps.title: Northstar partner meeting
meeting_preps.objective: Show progress since the last conversation and remove concern about pilot traction.
suggested_agenda: Open with May pilot metrics and what changed since the last deck.
Confirm whether Northstar wants deeper customer references before diligence.
Ask what would make LaurelinOS a clear seed-stage fit.
likely_questions: How much of the demo is local-first today versus future hosted onboarding?
What customer proof exists beyond the Atlas pilot?
How will BYOK and subscription-friendly compute affect margins?

meeting_preps.title: Beacon design-partner demo
meeting_preps.objective: Demonstrate source-scoped memory and approval-gated follow-up without using real customer data.
suggested_agenda: Show the daily brief generated from synthetic local artefacts.
Show open-loop detection with source IDs and no automatic indexing.
Ask which Beacon workflow would be worth testing with approved local sources later.
likely_questions: Can LaurelinOS avoid reading unrelated folders?
Can every suggestion cite a source artefact?
What happens before an external follow-up is sent?

daily_changes: Northstar requested revised deck and pilot metrics.
Engineering is blocked on calendar permission scope.
Beacon asked for a source-scoped meeting-prep demo.
Team decided to support customer subscriptions and BYOK before bundled compute.
